title: Some Useful Signal Operations
summary: This page illustrates time scaling operations on a signal x(t), demonstrating both time compression and time expansion. It shows that x(3t) compresses the signal by a factor of 3, while x(t/2) expands the signal by a factor of 2, with corresponding mathematical expressions and graphical representations. Key time instants are tracked to verify the scaling effects.

figures.caption: Illustration of time scaling of a signal x(t) showing the original signal (a), compressed version x_c(t) = x(3t/2) (b), and expanded version x_e(t) = x(t/2) (c).
